mission.title: external-secrets: Merge template data
mission.description: Merge template data. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Merge template data

steps.title: Check current Secret configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets resources:
```bash
kubectl get secret -A
kubectl describe secret <name> -n <namespace>
```
**Describe the solution you'd like**
On previous version of external-secrets, when a template is defined, it would merge the template with the secret keys.
For example, I'm using external-secrets to c

steps.title: Apply the fix for Merge template data
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

The Templating Mechanism had several issues regarding updates to the existing external Secret, or generally speaking, the ability of not being able to keep the data source with the template (essentially allowing the templating to also inject fixed values).

## Related Issue

##
```yaml
apiVersion: kubernetes-client.io/v1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  labels:
    argocd.argoproj.io/instance: argocd
  name: repo-k8s-conf
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  backendType: systemManager
  data:
  - key: /prod/devops/github/api_token
    name: password
  - key: /prod/devops/github/api_user
    name: username
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        managed-by: argocd.argoproj.io
      labels:
        argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repository
    stringData:
      type: git
      url: https://github.com/bla/k8s-conf.git
```

steps.title: Confirm Merge template data is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Problem Statement

The Templating Mechanism had several issues regarding updates to the existing external Secret, or generally speaking, the ability of not being able to keep the data source with the template (essentially allowing the templating to also inject fixed values).

## Related Issue

## Proposed Changes

This PR improves significantly the templating mechanism by also creating ManagedF. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
